rubric_items.description: Based on the instruction (not LAMMPS script), is the instruction intended to perform an NPT ensemble for 1.0 ns with 2 fs timestep (500000 steps) with hydrogen having a tritium-like mass?
Does the script define `units metal` and `atom_style atomic`? Any other `units` or `atom_style` are considered as False.
Does the LAMMPS input script write a restart file at the end using `write_restart <restart_filename>`?
Does the input explicitly include both `pair_style hybrid/overlay e3gnn d3 <parameter> <parameter> damp_bj pbe` and `pair_coeff * * d3 <element_list>`?
In `pair_coeff * *` lines, is the element order exactly: C F H Li O P ?

Does the script or the data file override Hydrogen mass (atom type 3) to a tritium-like value in the range [3.0, 3.03]? In LAMMPS, a typical examples are `mass 3 3.0`, `mass 3 3.016`, `mass 3 3.024`, `mass 3 3.0239999992220928`. If neither the data file nor the script modifies Hydrogen mass, set this as False.
Does the script initialize velocities at 298 K using `velocity all create 298 <seed> <options>`?
Does the script set the timestep to 0.002: either `timestep 0.002` or through a variable where it expands at read time to 0.002?
Does the script include a run command for 500000 steps: either `run 500000` or through a variable where it expands at read time to 500000? Timestep/units are irrelevant.

Does the script contain an NPT fix command that sets the temperature target to 298 K and uses the `iso` keyword? For example, `fix all npt temp 298.0 298.0 iso

`.

Does the script contain an NPT fix command that sets the temperature target to 298 K and does not use the `iso` keyword, but instead uses either the `aniso` keyword or separate axis-wise pressure control with `x`, `y`, and `z`? For example, `fix all npt temp 298.0 298.0 aniso

` or `fix all npt temp 298.0 298.0 x y z `.

Does the script contain an `npt/cauchy` fix command that sets the temperature target to 298 K? Accept `iso`, or `aniso` / separate `x`, `y`, and `z` pressure control only when all controlled diagonal pressure targets are identical and any shear components (`xy`, `xz`, `yz`) are absent or explicitly set to `0 0`. Reject unequal diagonal targets or any nonzero shear stress target. For example, `fix <id> all npt/cauchy temp 298.0 298.0 <Tdamp> iso 1.01325 1.01325 <Pdamp>` and `fix <id> all npt/cauchy temp 298.0 298.0 <Tdamp> x 1.0 1.0 <Pdamp> y 1.0 1.0 <Pdamp> z 1.0 1.0 <Pdamp> xy 0.0 0.0 <Pdamp> xz 0.0 0.0 <Pdamp> yz 0.0 0.0 <Pdamp>` are acceptable.

Does the script contain an NPT fix command that uses the `iso` keyword and sets the target pressure to a value between `1.0` and `1.0133` bar inclusive? For `iso`, both pressure entries must lie in this range. For example, `fix <id> all npt temp <Tstart> <Tstop> <Tdamp> iso 1.01325 1.01325 <Pdamp>` or `fix <id> all npt temp <Tstart> <Tstop> <Tdamp> iso 1.0 1.0 <Pdamp>`.

Does the script contain an NPT fix command that does not use the `iso` keyword, but instead uses either the `aniso` keyword or separate axis-wise pressure control with `x`, `y`, and `z`, and sets the target pressure to a value between `1.0` and `1.0133` bar inclusive on every controlled direction? For example, `fix <id> all npt temp <Tstart> <Tstop> <Tdamp> aniso 1.01325 1.01325 <Pdamp>` or `fix <id> all npt temp <Tstart> <Tstop> <Tdamp> x 1.0 1.0 <Pdamp> y 1.0 1.0 <Pdamp> z 1.0 1.0 <Pdamp>`.

Does the script contain an `npt/cauchy` fix command that applies a target pressure between `1.0` and `1.0133` bar inclusive? Accept `iso`, or `aniso` / separate `x`, `y`, and `z` pressure control only when all controlled diagonal pressure targets are identical and every controlled diagonal pressure entry lies in this range, while any shear components (`xy`, `xz`, `yz`) are absent or explicitly set to `0 0`. Reject unequal diagonal targets or any nonzero shear stress target. For example, `fix <id> all npt/cauchy temp <Tstart> <Tstop> <Tdamp> iso 1.01325 1.01325 <Pdamp>` and `fix <id> all npt/cauchy temp <Tstart> <Tstop> <Tdamp> x 1.0 1.0 <Pdamp> y 1.0 1.0 <Pdamp> z 1.0 1.0 <Pdamp> xy 0.0 0.0 <Pdamp> xz 0.0 0.0 <Pdamp> yz 0.0 0.0 <Pdamp>` are acceptable.

Does the script contain an NPT fix command that uses the `iso` keyword and explicitly specifies both a thermostat damping time and a barostat damping time? Numeric values and immediate expressions such as `$(100.0*dt)` are both acceptable. For example, `fix <id> all npt temp <Tstart> <Tstop> <Tdamp> iso <Pstart> <Pstop> <Pdamp>`.

Does the script contain an NPT fix command that does not use the `iso` keyword, but instead uses either the `aniso` keyword or separate axis-wise pressure control with `x`, `y`, and `z`, and explicitly specifies both a thermostat damping time and the required pressure damping time value or values? Numeric values and immediate expressions such as `$(100.0*dt)` are both acceptable. For example, `fix <id> all npt temp <Tstart> <Tstop> <Tdamp> aniso <Pstart> <Pstop> <Pdamp>` or `fix <id> all npt temp <Tstart> <Tstop> <Tdamp> x <Px1> <Px2> <Pdamp> y <Py1> <Py2> <Pdamp> z <Pz1> <Pz2> <Pdamp>`.

Does the script contain an `npt/cauchy` fix command that explicitly specifies both a thermostat damping time and the required pressure damping time value or values? Numeric values and immediate expressions such as `$(100.0*dt)` are both acceptable. Accept `iso`, or `aniso` / separate `x`, `y`, and `z` pressure control only when all controlled diagonal pressure targets are identical and any shear components (`xy`, `xz`, `yz`) are absent or explicitly set to `0 0`. Reject unequal diagonal targets or any nonzero shear stress target. For example, `fix <id> all npt/cauchy temp <Tstart> <Tstop> <Tdamp> iso 1.01325 1.01325 <Pdamp>` and `fix <id> all npt/cauchy temp <Tstart> <Tstop> <Tdamp> x 1.0 1.0 <Pdamp> y 1.0 1.0 <Pdamp> z 1.0 1.0 <Pdamp> xy 0.0 0.0 <Pdamp> xz 0.0 0.0 <Pdamp> yz 0.0 0.0 <Pdamp>` are acceptable.

Based on the instruction (not LAMMPS script), is the instruction intended to perform an NPT ensemble for 0.4 ns with 1 fs timestep (400000 steps) with normal hydrogen mass? Tritium-like hydrogen mass is not allowed.
Does the script include `read_restart <restart_filename>`?

Does the script either omit both `units` and `atom_style`, or explicitly define both as `units metal` and `atom_style atomic`? If either command is explicitly written with any other value, set this item as False.

Does the script explicitly define only one of the two commands of `units` or `atom_style`, with the command written correctly as either `units metal` or `atom_style atomic`, while the other command is omitted? If either command is explicitly written with any other value, set this item as False.
Does the script dump atomic trajectories in DCD format using `dump <dump_ID> all dcd 10 <dump_filename>`?
Does the LAMMPS input script reset the timestep to 0 with `reset_timestep 0`?
Does the input explicitly include both `pair_style hybrid/overlay e3gnn d3 <parameter> <parameter> damp_bj pbe` and `pair_coeff * * d3 <element_list>`?
In `pair_coeff * *` lines, is the element order exactly: C F H Li O P ?

Does the script set Hydrogen mass (atom type 3) to a normal hydrogen mass value in the range [1.0, 1.01]? In LAMMPS, typical examples are `mass 3 1.0`, `mass 3 1.008`, `mass 3 1.00784`, `mass 3 1.00794`.
If `timestep` command is used, does the script set the timestep to 0.001: either `timestep 0.001` or through a variable where it expands at read time to 0.001?
Does the script include a run command for 400000 steps: either `run 400000` or through a variable where it expands at read time to 400000? Timestep/units are irrelevant.

Does the script contain an NPT fix command that sets the temperature target to 298 K and uses the `iso` keyword? For example, `fix all npt temp 298.0 298.0 iso

`.

Does the script contain an NPT fix command that sets the temperature target to 298 K and does not use the `iso` keyword, but instead uses either the `aniso` keyword or separate axis-wise pressure control with `x`, `y`, and `z`? For example, `fix all npt temp 298.0 298.0 aniso

` or `fix all npt temp 298.0 298.0 x y z `.

Does the script contain an `npt/cauchy` fix command that sets the temperature target to 298 K? Accept `iso`, or `aniso` / separate `x`, `y`, and `z` pressure control only when all controlled diagonal pressure targets are identical and any shear components (`xy`, `xz`, `yz`) are absent or explicitly set to `0 0`. Reject unequal diagonal targets or any nonzero shear stress target. For example, `fix <id> all npt/cauchy temp 298.0 298.0 <Tdamp> iso 1.01325 1.01325 <Pdamp>` and `fix <id> all npt/cauchy temp 298.0 298.0 <Tdamp> x 1.0 1.0 <Pdamp> y 1.0 1.0 <Pdamp> z 1.0 1.0 <Pdamp> xy 0.0 0.0 <Pdamp> xz 0.0 0.0 <Pdamp> yz 0.0 0.0 <Pdamp>` are acceptable.

Does the script contain an NPT fix command that uses the `iso` keyword and sets the target pressure to a value between `1.0` and `1.0133` bar inclusive? For `iso`, both pressure entries must lie in this range. For example, `fix <id> all npt temp <Tstart> <Tstop> <Tdamp> iso 1.01325 1.01325 <Pdamp>` or `fix <id> all npt temp <Tstart> <Tstop> <Tdamp> iso 1.0 1.0 <Pdamp>`.

Does the script contain an NPT fix command that does not use the `iso` keyword, but instead uses either the `aniso` keyword or separate axis-wise pressure control with `x`, `y`, and `z`, and sets the target pressure to a value between `1.0` and `1.0133` bar inclusive on every controlled direction? For example, `fix <id> all npt temp <Tstart> <Tstop> <Tdamp> aniso 1.01325 1.01325 <Pdamp>` or `fix <id> all npt temp <Tstart> <Tstop> <Tdamp> x 1.0 1.0 <Pdamp> y 1.0 1.0 <Pdamp> z 1.0 1.0 <Pdamp>`.

Does the script contain an `npt/cauchy` fix command that applies a target pressure between `1.0` and `1.0133` bar inclusive? Accept `iso`, or `aniso` / separate `x`, `y`, and `z` pressure control only when all controlled diagonal pressure targets are identical and every controlled diagonal pressure entry lies in this range, while any shear components (`xy`, `xz`, `yz`) are absent or explicitly set to `0 0`. Reject unequal diagonal targets or any nonzero shear stress target. For example, `fix <id> all npt/cauchy temp <Tstart> <Tstop> <Tdamp> iso 1.01325 1.01325 <Pdamp>` and `fix <id> all npt/cauchy temp <Tstart> <Tstop> <Tdamp> x 1.0 1.0 <Pdamp> y 1.0 1.0 <Pdamp> z 1.0 1.0 <Pdamp> xy 0.0 0.0 <Pdamp> xz 0.0 0.0 <Pdamp> yz 0.0 0.0 <Pdamp>` are acceptable.

Does the script contain an NPT fix command that uses the `iso` keyword and explicitly specifies both a thermostat damping time and a barostat damping time? Numeric values and immediate expressions such as `$(100.0*dt)` are both acceptable. For example, `fix <id> all npt temp <Tstart> <Tstop> <Tdamp> iso <Pstart> <Pstop> <Pdamp>`.

Does the script contain an NPT fix command that does not use the `iso` keyword, but instead uses either the `aniso` keyword or separate axis-wise pressure control with `x`, `y`, and `z`, and explicitly specifies both a thermostat damping time and the required pressure damping time value or values? Numeric values and immediate expressions such as `$(100.0*dt)` are both acceptable. For example, `fix <id> all npt temp <Tstart> <Tstop> <Tdamp> aniso <Pstart> <Pstop> <Pdamp>` or `fix <id> all npt temp <Tstart> <Tstop> <Tdamp> x <Px1> <Px2> <Pdamp> y <Py1> <Py2> <Pdamp> z <Pz1> <Pz2> <Pdamp>`.

Does the script contain an `npt/cauchy` fix command that explicitly specifies both a thermostat damping time and the required pressure damping time value or values? Numeric values and immediate expressions such as `$(100.0*dt)` are both acceptable. Accept `iso`, or `aniso` / separate `x`, `y`, and `z` pressure control only when all controlled diagonal pressure targets are identical and any shear components (`xy`, `xz`, `yz`) are absent or explicitly set to `0 0`. Reject unequal diagonal targets or any nonzero shear stress target. For example, `fix <id> all npt/cauchy temp <Tstart> <Tstop> <Tdamp> iso 1.01325 1.01325 <Pdamp>` and `fix <id> all npt/cauchy temp <Tstart> <Tstop> <Tdamp> x 1.0 1.0 <Pdamp> y 1.0 1.0 <Pdamp> z 1.0 1.0 <Pdamp> xy 0.0 0.0 <Pdamp> xz 0.0 0.0 <Pdamp> yz 0.0 0.0 <Pdamp>` are acceptable.

Based on the instruction (not LAMMPS script), is the instruction intended to perform an NPT ensemble for 0.4 ns with 1 fs timestep (400000 steps) with normal hydrogen mass? Tritium-like hydrogen mass is not allowed.
Does the script include `read_data <data_filename>`?
Does the script define `units metal` and `atom_style atomic`? Any other `units` or `atom_style` are considered as False.
Does the script dump atomic trajectories in DCD format using `dump <dump_ID> all dcd 10 <dump_filename>`?
Does the input explicitly include both `pair_style hybrid/overlay e3gnn d3 <parameter> <parameter> damp_bj pbe` and `pair_coeff * * d3 <element_list>`?
In `pair_coeff * *` lines, is the element order exactly: C F H Li O P ?

Does the script set Hydrogen mass (atom type 3) to a normal hydrogen mass value in the range [1.0, 1.01]? In LAMMPS, typical examples are `mass 3 1.0`, `mass 3 1.008`, `mass 3 1.00784`, `mass 3 1.00794`. If the mass is already set to a value in this range in the loaded data file, set this as True.
Does the script initialize velocities at 298 K using `velocity all create 298 <seed> <options>`?
If `timestep` command is used, does the script set the timestep to 0.001: either `timestep 0.001` or through a variable where it expands at read time to 0.001?
Does the script include a run command for 400000 steps: either `run 400000` or through a variable where it expands at read time to 400000? Timestep/units are irrelevant.

Does the script contain an NPT fix command that sets the temperature target to 298 K and uses the `iso` keyword? For example, `fix all npt temp 298.0 298.0 iso

`.

Does the script contain an NPT fix command that sets the temperature target to 298 K and does not use the `iso` keyword, but instead uses either the `aniso` keyword or separate axis-wise pressure control with `x`, `y`, and `z`? For example, `fix all npt temp 298.0 298.0 aniso

` or `fix all npt temp 298.0 298.0 x y z `.

Does the script contain an `npt/cauchy` fix command that sets the temperature target to 298 K? Accept `iso`, or `aniso` / separate `x`, `y`, and `z` pressure control only when all controlled diagonal pressure targets are identical and any shear components (`xy`, `xz`, `yz`) are absent or explicitly set to `0 0`. Reject unequal diagonal targets or any nonzero shear stress target. For example, `fix <id> all npt/cauchy temp 298.0 298.0 <Tdamp> iso 1.01325 1.01325 <Pdamp>` and `fix <id> all npt/cauchy temp 298.0 298.0 <Tdamp> x 1.0 1.0 <Pdamp> y 1.0 1.0 <Pdamp> z 1.0 1.0 <Pdamp> xy 0.0 0.0 <Pdamp> xz 0.0 0.0 <Pdamp> yz 0.0 0.0 <Pdamp>` are acceptable.

Does the script contain an NPT fix command that uses the `iso` keyword and sets the target pressure to a value between `1.0` and `1.0133` bar inclusive? For `iso`, both pressure entries must lie in this range. For example, `fix <id> all npt temp <Tstart> <Tstop> <Tdamp> iso 1.01325 1.01325 <Pdamp>` or `fix <id> all npt temp <Tstart> <Tstop> <Tdamp> iso 1.0 1.0 <Pdamp>`.

Does the script contain an NPT fix command that does not use the `iso` keyword, but instead uses either the `aniso` keyword or separate axis-wise pressure control with `x`, `y`, and `z`, and sets the target pressure to a value between `1.0` and `1.0133` bar inclusive on every controlled direction? For example, `fix <id> all npt temp <Tstart> <Tstop> <Tdamp> aniso 1.01325 1.01325 <Pdamp>` or `fix <id> all npt temp <Tstart> <Tstop> <Tdamp> x 1.0 1.0 <Pdamp> y 1.0 1.0 <Pdamp> z 1.0 1.0 <Pdamp>`.

Does the script contain an `npt/cauchy` fix command that applies a target pressure between `1.0` and `1.0133` bar inclusive? Accept `iso`, or `aniso` / separate `x`, `y`, and `z` pressure control only when all controlled diagonal pressure targets are identical and every controlled diagonal pressure entry lies in this range, while any shear components (`xy`, `xz`, `yz`) are absent or explicitly set to `0 0`. Reject unequal diagonal targets or any nonzero shear stress target. For example, `fix <id> all npt/cauchy temp <Tstart> <Tstop> <Tdamp> iso 1.01325 1.01325 <Pdamp>` and `fix <id> all npt/cauchy temp <Tstart> <Tstop> <Tdamp> x 1.0 1.0 <Pdamp> y 1.0 1.0 <Pdamp> z 1.0 1.0 <Pdamp> xy 0.0 0.0 <Pdamp> xz 0.0 0.0 <Pdamp> yz 0.0 0.0 <Pdamp>` are acceptable.

Does the script contain an NPT fix command that uses the `iso` keyword and explicitly specifies both a thermostat damping time and a barostat damping time? Numeric values and immediate expressions such as `$(100.0*dt)` are both acceptable. For example, `fix <id> all npt temp <Tstart> <Tstop> <Tdamp> iso <Pstart> <Pstop> <Pdamp>`.

Does the script contain an NPT fix command that does not use the `iso` keyword, but instead uses either the `aniso` keyword or separate axis-wise pressure control with `x`, `y`, and `z`, and explicitly specifies both a thermostat damping time and the required pressure damping time value or values? Numeric values and immediate expressions such as `$(100.0*dt)` are both acceptable. For example, `fix <id> all npt temp <Tstart> <Tstop> <Tdamp> aniso <Pstart> <Pstop> <Pdamp>` or `fix <id> all npt temp <Tstart> <Tstop> <Tdamp> x <Px1> <Px2> <Pdamp> y <Py1> <Py2> <Pdamp> z <Pz1> <Pz2> <Pdamp>`.

Does the script contain an `npt/cauchy` fix command that explicitly specifies both a thermostat damping time and the required pressure damping time value or values? Numeric values and immediate expressions such as `$(100.0*dt)` are both acceptable. Accept `iso`, or `aniso` / separate `x`, `y`, and `z` pressure control only when all controlled diagonal pressure targets are identical and any shear components (`xy`, `xz`, `yz`) are absent or explicitly set to `0 0`. Reject unequal diagonal targets or any nonzero shear stress target. For example, `fix <id> all npt/cauchy temp <Tstart> <Tstop> <Tdamp> iso 1.01325 1.01325 <Pdamp>` and `fix <id> all npt/cauchy temp <Tstart> <Tstop> <Tdamp> x 1.0 1.0 <Pdamp> y 1.0 1.0 <Pdamp> z 1.0 1.0 <Pdamp> xy 0.0 0.0 <Pdamp> xz 0.0 0.0 <Pdamp> yz 0.0 0.0 <Pdamp>` are acceptable.
Based on the instruction (not LAMMPS script), is the instruction intended to perform an NVT run for 1.1 ns with center of mass drift addressed? (1100000 steps)
Does the script define `units metal` and `atom_style atomic`? Any other `units` or `atom_style` are considered as False.
Does the script dump atomic trajectories in DCD format using `dump <dump_ID> all dcd 100 <dump_filename>`?
Does the script explicitly unwrap coordinates with `dump_modify <dump_ID> unwrap yes`?
Does the LAMMPS input script write a restart file at the end using `write_restart <restart_filename>`?
Does the input explicitly include both `pair_style hybrid/overlay e3gnn d3 <parameter> <parameter> damp_bj pbe` and `pair_coeff * * d3 <element_list>`?
In `pair_coeff * *` lines, is the element order exactly: C F H Li O P ?
Does the script initialize velocities at 298 K using `velocity all create 298 <seed> <options>`?
Does the script apply a fix to remove center-of-mass drift using `fix <id> all momentum <frequency> linear 1 1 1`? The frequency should be lower than 1000.
If `timestep` command is used, does the script set the timestep to 0.001: either `timestep 0.001` or through a variable where it expands at read time to 0.001?
Does the script include a run command for 1100000 steps: either `run 1100000` or through a variable where it expands at read time to 1100000? Timestep/units are irrelevant.
Is the simulation temperature set to 298 K `fix <id> all nvt temp 298 298 <thermostat_damping_constant>`?

Are thermostat damping times explicitly set in the `fix <id> all nvt temp <temperature> <temperature> <thermostat_damping_constant>` command? Numeric values (0.01) and immediate expressions such as `$(100.0*dt)` are both fine.

Is the chosen ensemble NVT using `fix <id> all nvt temp <temperature> <temperature> <thermostat_damping_constant>`? If the fix command includes any pressure keywords or barostat damping terms, set this as False.
